fn pick(a: A): A / {} { match a { x => x } }
---
pick : fn(A) -> A
==========
data OptionA { .some(A), .none }
fn pick2(a: OptionA, b: A): A / {} { match a { .some(let x) => x, .none => b } }
---
pick2 : fn(OptionA, A) -> A
==========
data Option { .some(A), .none }
fn bad(a: Option): Option / {} { match a { .some(let x, let y) => a, .none => a } }
---
ERROR: pattern `some` expects 1 args, got 2
==========
data Option { .some(A), .none }
fn bad2(a: Option): Option / {} { match a { .nope => a } }
---
ERROR: unknown variant `nope` in match pattern
==========
data OptionInt { .some(B), .none }
fn ok(a: OptionInt, b: B): B / {} { match a { .some(x) => x, .none => b } }
---
ok : fn(OptionInt, B) -> B
==========
data OptionInt { .some(B), .none }
fn bad3(a: OptionInt, c: C): B / {} { match a { .some(x) => c, .none => c } }
---
ERROR: type mismatch: expected B, got C
==========
data Pair { .pair(B, C) }
data Wrap { .wrap(Pair), .empty }
fn nested_ok(w: Wrap, b: B): B / {} { match w { .wrap(.pair(x, _)) => x, .empty => b } }
---
nested_ok : fn(Wrap, B) -> B
==========
data Pair { .pair(B, C) }
data Wrap { .wrap(Pair), .empty }
fn nested_bad(w: Wrap, b: B): B / {} { match w { .wrap(.nope(x)) => b, .empty => b } }
---
ERROR: unknown variant `nope` in match pattern
==========
data Bool { .true, .false }
fn ne(b: Bool): Bool / {} { match b { .true => b } }
---
ERROR: non-exhaustive match: missing patterns .false
==========
data Bool { .true, .false }
fn ex(b: Bool): Bool / {} { match b { .true => b, _ => b } }
---
ex : fn(Bool) -> Bool
==========
data Bool { .true, .false }
fn unreach1(b: Bool): Bool / {} { match b { _ => b, .true => b } }
---
ERROR: unreachable match arm: pattern already covered
==========
data Bool { .true, .false }
fn unreach2(b: Bool): Bool / {} { match b { .true => b, .true => b, .false => b } }
---
ERROR: unreachable match arm: pattern already covered
==========
data Bool { .true, .false }
data Nat { .zero, .succ(Nat) }
fn is_even(n: Nat, t: Bool, f: Bool): Bool / {} { match n { .zero => t, .succ(.zero) => f, .succ(.succ(let n)) => is_even(n, t, f) } }
---
is_even : fn(Nat, Bool, Bool) -> Bool
==========
data Bool { .true, .false }
data Nat { .zero, .succ(Nat) }
fn f(n: Nat, b: Bool): Bool / {} { match n { .succ(.zero) => b } }
---
ERROR: non-exhaustive match: missing patterns .succ(.succ(_)), .zero
